title: rubus schmidelyanus subsp. amictus
intro: Rubus schmidelyanus subsp. amictus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Indian raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus schmidelyanus plant, belonging to the Rosaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus schmidelyanus subsp. amictus include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through various preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be consumed as teas, tinctures, or added to food products.
